title: Erigeron thermarum
intro: Erigeron thermarum, also known as the hot spring daisy, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron thermarum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Erigeron thermarum include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote wound healing.
